artifact: Dao De Jing 51 — Way Gives Birth
english: The Way gives birth; virtue nourishes.
spanish: El Camino engendra; la virtud nutre.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 51
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 52 — Guard the Mother
english: The world has a beginning; take it as the mother of the world.
spanish: El mundo tiene un comienzo; tómalo como madre del mundo.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 52
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 53 — Great Way Is Level
english: The Great Way is very level, yet people love side paths.
spanish: El Gran Camino es muy llano, pero la gente ama los atajos.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 53
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

original: 善建者不拔，善抱者不脫。
english: What is well planted is not uprooted; what is well held does not slip away.
spanish: Lo bien plantado no se arranca; lo bien sostenido no se escapa.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 54
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: The one filled with virtue is like a newborn child.
spanish: Quien está lleno de virtud es como un recién nacido.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 55
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 56 — Shut the Gate
english: Those who know do not speak; those who speak do not know.
spanish: Quienes saben no hablan; quienes hablan no saben.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 56
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 57 — Govern with Straightness
original: 以正治國，以奇用兵，以無事取天下。
english: Govern with uprightness; use surprise in war; take the world by not meddling.
spanish: Gobierna con rectitud; usa sorpresa en la guerra; toma el mundo sin entrometerte.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 57
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 58 — Fortune and Misfortune
original: 禍兮福之所倚，福兮禍之所伏。
english: Misfortune is where fortune leans; fortune is where misfortune hides.
spanish: La desgracia es donde se apoya la fortuna; la fortuna es donde se oculta la desgracia.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 58
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

original: 深根固柢，長生久視之道。
english: Deep root and firm base: the way of long life and enduring vision.
spanish: Raíz profunda y base firme: el camino de larga vida y visión duradera.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 59
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: Governing a great country is like cooking a small fish.
spanish: Gobernar un gran país es como cocinar un pez pequeño.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 60
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: A great state is the low stream toward which waters flow.
spanish: Un gran estado es la corriente baja hacia donde fluyen las aguas.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 61
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 62 — Treasure of the World
english: The Way is the hidden treasure of the ten thousand things.
spanish: El Camino es el tesoro oculto de las diez mil cosas.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 62
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 63 — Difficult While Easy
original: 圖難於其易，為大於其細。
english: Plan the difficult while it is easy; do the great while it is small.
spanish: Planifica lo difícil mientras es fácil; haz lo grande mientras es pequeño.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 63
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

original: 九層之臺，起於累土；千里之行，始於足下。
english: A nine-story tower rises from piled earth; a thousand-mile journey begins beneath the feet.
spanish: Una torre de nueve pisos se alza desde tierra acumulada; un viaje de mil millas empieza bajo los pies.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 64
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

original: 古之善為道者，非以明民，將以愚之。
english: Those skilled in the ancient Way did not sharpen people with cleverness; they returned them to simplicity.
spanish: Quienes eran diestros en el antiguo Camino no afilaban al pueblo con astucia; lo devolvían a la sencillez.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 65
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 66 — Below the Rivers
original: 江海所以能為百谷王者，以其善下之。
english: Rivers and seas rule the valleys because they are good at staying below them.
spanish: Ríos y mares gobiernan los valles porque saben permanecer debajo de ellos.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 66
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: I have three treasures; I hold and guard them.
spanish: Tengo tres tesoros; los sostengo y los guardo.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 67
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: One skilled in battle does not become angry.
spanish: Quien es diestro en la batalla no se enoja.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 68
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 69 — Guest, Not Host
english: I dare not act as host; I act as guest.
spanish: No me atrevo a actuar como anfitrión; actúo como huésped.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 69
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 70 — Easy to Know
english: My words are very easy to know, very easy to practice.
spanish: Mis palabras son muy fáciles de conocer, muy fáciles de practicar.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 70
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

original: 知不知，尚矣；不知知，病也。
english: To know not-knowing is highest; not to know while thinking one knows is sickness.
spanish: Saber el no-saber es lo más alto; no saber creyendo saber es enfermedad.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 71
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 72 — Do Not Press
english: When people no longer fear power, a greater power arrives.
spanish: Cuando el pueblo ya no teme al poder, llega un poder mayor.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 72
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: Heaven's net is vast; its mesh is wide, yet nothing is lost.
spanish: La red del cielo es vasta; su malla es amplia, pero nada se pierde.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 73
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: There is always an executioner who does the killing.
spanish: Siempre hay un ejecutor que realiza la muerte.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 74
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

original: 民之饑，以其上食稅之多。
english: People starve because those above consume too much in taxes.
spanish: El pueblo pasa hambre porque los de arriba consumen demasiado en impuestos.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 75
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 76 — Soft and Tender
original: 人之生也柔弱，其死也堅強。
english: At birth a person is soft and weak; at death hard and rigid.
spanish: Al nacer una persona es blanda y débil; al morir, dura y rígida.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 76
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: The Way of heaven is like drawing a bow.
spanish: El Camino del cielo es como tensar un arco.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 77
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

artifact: Dao De Jing 78 — Nothing Softer than Water
original: 天下莫柔弱於水，而攻堅強者莫之能勝。
english: Nothing under heaven is softer than water, yet nothing surpasses it against the hard and strong.
spanish: Nada bajo el cielo es más blando que el agua, y nada la supera contra lo duro y fuerte.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 78
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: Reconcile a great resentment, and some resentment will remain.
spanish: Reconcilia un gran resentimiento, y algo de resentimiento quedará.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 79
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: A small country, few people.
spanish: Un país pequeño, poca gente.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 80
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.

english: True words are not ornate; ornate words are not true.
spanish: Las palabras verdaderas no son ornamentadas; las ornamentadas no son verdaderas.
recommendedEditionSource: Chinese Text Project — Dao De Jing 81
notes: Mode A queue. Canonical Chinese anchor; working English/Spanish rendering.
